steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Report the CEMS measurements to the agency in units of µg/m3 on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.

preconditions.text: Before conducting a RA test, the CEMS must be properly installed and prepared for operation according to the manufacturer's written instructions.

If the cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location and a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established, the Administrator may require the CEMS to be relocated.
